title: Discovery-surface updates: START_HERE / ROLE_SESSION_ORCHESTRATION / ORCHESTRATOR_MANAGED_WORKFLOW_PLAYBOOK
goal: Update the three operator-facing discovery surfaces so any incoming model or operator finds the HBR system on first read. START_HERE adds a top-section pointer to HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json + CX-131. ROLE_SESSION_ORCHESTRATION documents the HandoffGate (MT-004) as a mandatory transition. ORCHESTRATOR_MANAGED_WORKFLOW_PLAYBOOK adds the HBR matrix-check sequence to its run-through.
expected_diff_shape: Edit existing files under .GOV/operator/ or .GOV/discovery/ (confirm via Glob); additive edits preserving all existing content; new HBR-pointer subsections.
unblock_conditions: Role protocols updated (MT-038), templates updated (MT-039), swarm invariants test passing (MT-037 — proves the whole A.5 chain is live so the playbook can reference it confidently).
implementation_notes: Confirm file locations via Glob before editing. Likely paths: `.GOV/discovery/`, `.GOV/operator/discovery/`, or `.GOV/operator/`. START_HERE.md: add a top-of-file section `## Build Rules (HBR)` pointing to HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json + spec §5.6 + CX-131; mention that every WP touching product code must satisfy the applicable HBR rows per CX-503B1. ROLE_SESSION_ORCHESTRATION.md: add a section `## HBR Handoff Gate` documenting that every governed inter-role transition (refinement->coder, coder->WP_VALIDATOR, WP_VALIDATOR->INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR, INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR->ORCHESTRATOR) fires the HandoffGate (MT-004) and blocks on failure; cite the role-protocol links from MT-038. ORCHESTRATOR_MANAGED_WORKFLOW_PLAYBOOK.md: add a step to the standard run-through `Step <N>: Run \`just hbr-matrix-check\` before any coder handoff` and `Step <N+1>: Run \`just hbr-visual-smoke\` + \`just hbr-swarm-n8\` + \`just hbr-inspector-smoke\` before INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR closeout`. All three are *additive* — preserve every existing section and ordering. If the playbook does not have a numbered steps section, document the discovery and propose the additions as a new section instead of forcing them into a non-existent structure. Tests: a Node check `.GOV/roles_shared/checks/discovery-hbr-pointers.mjs` greps each of the three discovery files for the required HBR pointers; gov-check sub-check. This is the FINAL MT for cluster A; on completion, every operator + model entry point surfaces the HBR system within the first scroll. NOTE: This MT depends on MT-037 (the swarm invariants test) because the playbook references the recipe by name and the recipe must work.
proof_commands: just hbr-matrix-check && just hbr-visual-smoke && just hbr-swarm-n8 && just hbr-inspector-smoke
risk_if_missed: Without discovery-surface updates, operators and no-context models still bypass HBR on first read; the system's value is invisible.
validator_focus: All three discovery surfaces include the HBR pointers; additive edits preserve existing content; recipe names cited in the playbook exist in the justfile.

minimum_controls: Discovery edits are additive (no removed content).
Recipe names cited in the playbook are validated to exist in the justfile.
If discovery files do not exist at expected paths, MT documents the discovery and proposes alternates rather than fabricating.
